question_text: Which of the following correctly identifies the difference between a 'professional player' and an 'amateur player' in the context of sports participation and competition?
options: A. There is no difference; both terms refer to the same category of sports participants

B. A professional player earns a livelihood by playing sport as a primary occupation, while an amateur player participates in sport for personal enjoyment, fitness or passion without it being their primary source of income
C. An amateur player is always more skilled than a professional player in every sport
D. A professional player is one who has never received any formal training in the sport
explanation: A professional player plays sport as their primary occupation and earns their livelihood through match fees, contracts, endorsements and prize money. An amateur player participates in sport for personal enjoyment, fitness, passion or recreation without it being their main source of income. This distinction has historical roots in the Olympic movement.
elimination_hints: A denies an established distinction; C reverses skill expectations; D contradicts the reality that professionals undergo extensive training.
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: Which of the following government schemes launched by the Sports Authority of India is specifically designed to identify and nurture young sporting talent from grassroots level across the country?
options: A. Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana, which focuses on affordable housing in urban areas
B. SAI Training Centre (STC) scheme and National Sports Talent Contest (NSTC), which identify talented young athletes and provide them systematic training, coaching, equipment and financial support
C. Swachh Bharat Mission, which focuses on sanitation and cleanliness across India
D. Digital India Programme, which promotes electronic governance and digital infrastructure
explanation: SAI runs several talent development schemes: SAI Training Centres (STCs) in various states provide systematic coaching to identified young athletes, while the National Sports Talent Contest (NSTC) identifies talent at school level through competitions. Selected athletes receive coaching, equipment, diet allowance and competition exposure.
elimination_hints: A, C and D are government schemes unrelated to sports. Only B describes SAI's specific talent identification and nurturing programmes.
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: Analyze the role of a 'sports commentator' as a career in Physical Education and identify which combination of skills is most essential for success in this profession.
options: A. Advanced laboratory research skills in biochemistry and molecular biology

B. In-depth knowledge of the sport, excellent verbal communication and articulation, ability to analyse play in real time, and capacity to engage the audience with spontaneous and insightful commentary
C. Expertise in financial accounting and tax compliance for sports organisations
D. Skill in operating heavy construction machinery for building sports stadiums
explanation: A sports commentator needs deep knowledge of the sport (rules, strategies, player histories), excellent verbal communication and articulation, the ability to analyse and interpret play in real time, and the talent to keep audiences engaged with spontaneous, informative and entertaining commentary. It is a recognised PE-related media career.
elimination_hints: A, C and D describe unrelated professions. B correctly identifies the specific skill combination for sports commentary.
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: Evaluate the significance of the development of coaching systems and training facilities by organisations like SAI and NSNIS in improving India's performance at international multi-sport events like the Olympic Games and Asian Games.
options: A. These organisations have had no impact because India's international performance depends entirely on individual talent without any institutional support

B. SAI and NSNIS have significantly improved India's international performance by providing world-class coaching, scientific training methods, sports science support, infrastructure and competitive exposure that help transform raw talent into medal-winning athletes
C. India's improved performance is solely due to private academies, and government organisations like SAI and NSNIS play no role whatsoever
D. NSNIS and SAI have only focused on cricket, which is why India performs well only in cricket at international events
explanation: SAI and NSNIS have made significant contributions to India's international performance: NSNIS trains coaches who then develop athletes nationally; SAI provides infrastructure, training centres, scientific support (biomechanics, nutrition, sports medicine) and competitive exposure. Many Olympic and Asian Games medallists have trained at SAI centres with NSNIS-certified coaches.
elimination_hints: A ignores documented institutional contributions; C wrongly excludes government organisations; D falsely claims cricket-only focus (both serve multiple sports).
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: A person who designs and manufactures sports equipment such as cricket bats, footballs, badminton racquets and athletic shoes is pursuing a career in which area related to Physical Education?
options: A. Sports journalism and broadcasting
B. Sports equipment manufacturing and marketing
C. Sports psychology and mental training
D. Sports officiating and refereeing
explanation: Designing and manufacturing sports equipment (bats, balls, racquets, shoes) falls under the career area of sports equipment manufacturing and marketing. This is a recognised career path related to Physical Education that combines engineering, design and sports knowledge.
elimination_hints: A involves media; C involves psychology; D involves game officiating. Only B relates to equipment production.
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: Which of the following correctly explains the primary objective of the NSNIS Patiala in the context of developing India's coaching ecosystem across various sports disciplines?
options: A. NSNIS aims to produce only international athletes and does not focus on coaching development

B. NSNIS aims to train and certify coaches in various sports disciplines through specialised diploma programmes, develop coaching methodology, conduct research in sports science, and upgrade the quality of coaching across India
C. NSNIS functions exclusively as a sports museum displaying historical sports artefacts from India
D. NSNIS focuses only on traditional Indian sports and does not offer coaching in Olympic disciplines
explanation: NSNIS Patiala's primary objective is developing India's coaching ecosystem: it offers specialised coaching diploma courses in various Olympic and non-Olympic sports, develops standardised coaching methodology, conducts sports science research, and provides refresher courses to upgrade coaching quality nationwide.
elimination_hints: A misidentifies the focus (coaches, not athletes); C and D are factually incorrect descriptions of NSNIS.
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: Analyze the challenges faced by aspiring sports professionals in India who wish to pursue a full-time career as a professional sportsperson, and identify which combination of factors presents the most significant barriers.
options: A. India has no challenges at all and every aspiring athlete achieves professional success easily

B. Limited grassroots infrastructure in many regions, financial constraints for families to support long-term training, societal pressure to pursue conventional academic careers, inadequate sports science support in smaller cities, and limited competitive exposure at the international level during formative years
C. The only challenge is the lack of cricket coaching centres, as all other sports have excellent infrastructure
D. Professional sports careers face no financial barriers because the government fully funds all athletes from age five onward
explanation: Aspiring sports professionals in India face multiple interconnected challenges: limited grassroots infrastructure (especially outside metro cities), financial burden on families for long-term training/travel/equipment, societal preference for conventional academic/professional careers, inadequate sports science facilities in smaller cities, and limited early international competitive exposure.
elimination_hints: A denies obvious challenges; C ignores multi-sport issues; D overstates government funding reality. B comprehensively identifies the actual barriers.
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: Which of the following best describes the role of national sports federations in India, such as the Athletics Federation of India (AFI) or Hockey India, in the development and governance of their respective sports?
options: A. National federations are commercial entertainment companies that produce sports-themed movies and television shows

B. National federations govern their respective sports by organising national championships, selecting national teams, implementing rules, developing grassroots programmes, affiliating with international federations, and managing anti-doping compliance
C. National federations only sell tickets for international matches and have no governance role
D. National federations are the same as state governments and perform all administrative functions of the state
explanation: National sports federations (AFI, Hockey India, BAI, AIFF etc.) are the governing bodies for their respective sports in India. They organise national championships, select national teams, implement rules aligned with international federations (World Athletics, FIH etc.), develop grassroots programmes, manage anti-doping compliance and represent India internationally.
elimination_hints: A, C and D mischaracterise federations. B correctly describes the comprehensive governance and development role of national sports federations.
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: Evaluate why the career of a 'gym instructor' or 'personal fitness trainer' has witnessed significant growth in Indian metropolitan cities over the past decade, and identify the primary driving factors behind this trend.
options: A. The career has not grown at all and gym instructors remain extremely rare in India

B. Rising health consciousness, increasing prevalence of lifestyle diseases, growing disposable income, social media fitness culture, and the expansion of commercial fitness chains have collectively driven demand for qualified gym instructors and personal trainers in urban India
C. Gym instructors are in demand only because government laws mandate every citizen to visit a gymnasium daily
D. The growth is entirely due to international gym chains and Indian professionals have no role in this sector
explanation: Multiple converging factors have driven growth: rising health awareness and lifestyle disease concerns (diabetes, obesity), growing urban disposable income, social media fitness influencer culture, corporate wellness programmes, and the expansion of commercial gym chains (Gold's Gym, Cult.fit, Anytime Fitness). These have created strong demand for qualified fitness professionals.
elimination_hints: A contradicts observable reality; C invents a non-existent law; D wrongly excludes Indian professionals (most trainers are Indian).
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: Analyze how the Khelo India programme launched by the Government of India contributes to both talent identification and career development in sports at the grassroots level across the country.
options: A. Khelo India has no talent identification component and only provides sports equipment to schools

B. Khelo India identifies talented young athletes through school, university and state-level competitions, provides them annual scholarships for training, education and living expenses, and creates a structured pathway from grassroots participation to national and international competition, thus supporting career development in sports
C. Khelo India is limited to organising a single annual event and does not provide any financial support to athletes
D. Khelo India operates only in Delhi and has no presence in other states across India
explanation: Khelo India identifies talented athletes through multi-tier competitions (school, university, state level), awards annual scholarships (Rs. 5 lakh per athlete per year for 8 years covering training, education and living expenses), develops sports infrastructure, and creates a structured pathway from grassroots to elite competition — directly supporting career development in sports.
elimination_hints: A ignores the scholarship component; C understates the programme's scope; D wrongly limits it to Delhi (it operates nationwide).
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: Match the following career professionals in Column A with the specific educational qualification or certification most directly required for their role in Column B.
column_a: 1. Physical Education teacher in a CBSE school
2. Certified sports coach at a SAI training centre
4. Sports journalist writing for a national newspaper
column_b: P. Bachelor's degree in Physiotherapy (BPT) with specialisation in sports rehabilitation
Q. BPEd or MPEd degree along with CTET/State TET qualification
R. Coaching diploma from NSNIS Patiala or recognised equivalent institution
S. Degree in Journalism/Mass Communication with strong sports knowledge
explanation: PE teacher in CBSE school → BPEd/MPEd + TET (Q). SAI coach → NSNIS coaching diploma (R). Sports physiotherapist → BPT degree (P). Sports journalist → Journalism degree + sports knowledge (S).
elimination_hints: PE teacher (1) needs BPEd + TET (Q); physiotherapist (3) needs BPT (P). These two are the most confident matches to anchor the answer.
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: Match the following SAI centres or schemes in Column A with their correct location or purpose in Column B.
column_b: P. Patiala — also houses NSNIS, focuses on coaching and athlete development in northern region
Q. Kolkata — focuses on sports development and training in the eastern region of India
R. Bengaluru — focuses on sports training and development for southern region athletes
S. A nationwide scheme to identify sporting talent among school-age children through competitions
explanation: SAI Southern Centre → Bengaluru (R). SAI Northern Centre → Patiala with NSNIS (P). SAI Eastern Centre → Kolkata (Q). NSTC → nationwide talent identification scheme (S).
elimination_hints: Northern Centre (2) is in Patiala alongside NSNIS (P) — the most well-known fact. NSTC (4) is a scheme, not a location (S).
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: Match the following factors influencing sports development in Column A with their specific impact on career opportunities in Physical Education in Column B.
column_a: 1. Growth of digital streaming platforms (OTT) for sports
2. Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) spending on sports by Indian companies
3. Expansion of sports science departments in Indian universities
column_b: P. Creates academic and research career opportunities for sports scientists, biomechanists and exercise physiologists
Q. Creates career opportunities in sports digital content production, streaming technology and sports data analytics
R. Provides customised training support and financial assistance to elite athletes identified as potential Olympic medallists
S. Funds grassroots sports infrastructure, academies and community sports programmes, creating jobs for coaches and administrators
explanation: OTT platforms → digital content and analytics careers (Q). CSR spending → grassroots infrastructure and coaching jobs (S). University sports science departments → academic and research careers (P). TOPS → elite athlete support for Olympic medals (R).
elimination_hints: OTT/digital platforms (1) clearly relate to digital content careers (Q); TOPS (4) is specifically about Olympic medal hopefuls (R).
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: Read the Assertion and Reason below and select the correct option regarding the IOC and the Olympic Movement.
assertion: The International Olympic Committee (IOC) plays a central role in promoting sports worldwide by organising the Olympic Games and supporting national Olympic committees in member countries.
reason: The IOC was founded by Pierre de Coubertin in 1894 with the vision of reviving the ancient Olympic Games and promoting international understanding through sports.
options: A. Both Assertion and Reason are true, and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion
B. Both Assertion and Reason are true, but Reason is NOT the correct explanation of Assertion
C. Assertion is true but Reason is false
D. Assertion is false but Reason is true
explanation: The Assertion is true — the IOC promotes sports worldwide through the Olympics and support for NOCs. The Reason is also true and explains the foundational vision: Pierre de Coubertin founded the IOC in 1894 specifically to revive the Olympics and promote international understanding — this founding purpose is why the IOC plays its central role today.
elimination_hints: Both statements are well-established historical facts. The Reason provides the founding context and vision that explain why the IOC exists and functions as described in the Assertion.
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: Read the Assertion and Reason below and select the correct option regarding the career of sports equipment manufacturing in India.
assertion: The sports equipment manufacturing industry in India has significant career potential due to India being one of the world's largest producers of sporting goods, particularly in centres like Jalandhar (Punjab) and Meerut (Uttar Pradesh).
reason: India imports all its sports equipment from China and does not manufacture any sporting goods domestically.
options: A. Both Assertion and Reason are true, and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion
B. Both Assertion and Reason are true, but Reason is NOT the correct explanation of Assertion
C. Assertion is true but Reason is false
D. Assertion is false but Reason is true
explanation: The Assertion is true — India is a major global producer of sporting goods, with established manufacturing hubs in Jalandhar (footballs, hockey sticks) and Meerut (cricket bats, boxing equipment). The Reason is false — India does not import all equipment from China; it has a thriving domestic manufacturing industry that also exports globally.
elimination_hints: The Reason's claim that India 'does not manufacture any sporting goods' directly contradicts the Assertion's mention of Jalandhar and Meerut as manufacturing centres, making it clearly false.
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: Read the Assertion and Reason below and select the correct option regarding the importance of sports for differently-abled individuals in career development.
assertion: The Paralympic Games and Special Olympics have opened new career opportunities for differently-abled athletes as professional sportspersons, coaches and motivational speakers.
reason: International recognition of para-sports has increased sponsorship, media coverage and government support for differently-abled athletes, making sports a viable career path for them.
options: A. Both Assertion and Reason are true, and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion
B. Both Assertion and Reason are true, but Reason is NOT the correct explanation of Assertion
C. Assertion is true but Reason is false
D. Assertion is false but Reason is true
explanation: The Assertion is true — Paralympics and Special Olympics have opened career paths for differently-abled athletes. The Reason is also true and explains how: increased international recognition has brought sponsorship, media exposure and government support, making para-sports careers financially sustainable.
elimination_hints: Both statements are supported by the growth of para-sports globally. The Reason explains the financial and support mechanism that makes the Assertion's career opportunities possible.
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: Arrange the following levels of sports competition in India in the correct hierarchical order from the lowest grassroots level to the highest national level.
items: I. National-level championship organised by the national sports federation
II. Inter-school tournament at the district level
III. State-level championship involving district winners from across the state
IV. Zonal or divisional competition involving winners from multiple districts
options: A. II → IV → III → I
B. I → III → IV → II
C. III → I → II → IV
D. IV → II → I → III
explanation: Competition hierarchy (grassroots to national): District inter-school (II) → Zonal/divisional (IV, combines districts) → State championship (III, combines zones) → National championship (I, combines states). This is the standard pyramid structure for talent progression in Indian sports.
elimination_hints: District inter-school (II) is clearly the lowest level; national championship (I) is clearly the highest. Anchor with these endpoints.
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: Arrange the following milestones in the evolution of sports media in India in the correct chronological order from earliest to most recent.
items: I. Launch of digital OTT platforms streaming live sports events in India
II. Radio commentary of cricket matches becoming popular across India
III. Launch of dedicated 24-hour sports television channels in India
IV. Social media platforms enabling athletes to directly engage with fans and build personal brands
options: A. II → III → I → IV
B. III → I → IV → II
C. I → IV → II → III
D. IV → II → III → I
explanation: Sports media evolution: Radio cricket commentary (II, 1930s–1960s era) → Dedicated 24/7 sports TV channels (III, 1990s–2000s era) → Digital OTT sports streaming (I, 2010s) → Social media personal branding by athletes (IV, mid-2010s onward). Radio preceded TV, TV preceded digital streaming, and social media athlete branding is the most recent development.
elimination_hints: Radio commentary (II) is the oldest form of sports media; social media branding (IV) is the most recent. Anchor with these.
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: True or False: The Indian Olympic Association (IOA) is the national Olympic committee of India and is responsible for selecting and managing the Indian contingent for the Olympic Games, Asian Games and Commonwealth Games.
options: A. True
B. False
explanation: This statement is True. The IOA (established 1927) is India's national Olympic committee, affiliated with the IOC. It selects athletes, manages Indian contingents for multi-sport events (Olympics, Asian Games, Commonwealth Games), and oversees national sports federations.
elimination_hints: This is a standard factual statement about the IOA's constitutional role — selection, management and multi-sport event representation are all correct.
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: Study the diagram below that maps the organisational hierarchy of sports administration in India, showing relationships between the Ministry, SAI, IOA, national federations and state associations. Based on the diagram, through which body does the Government of India primarily channel its funding and policy implementation for sports development across the country?
options: A. Through individual state governments only, with no central coordination mechanism

B. Through the Sports Authority of India (SAI), which functions as the primary implementing agency under the Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports for managing infrastructure, running training programmes and disbursing government sports development funds
C. Through private corporate sponsors who manage all government sports funds independently
D. Through the International Olympic Committee (IOC), which distributes funds to all Indian athletes directly
explanation: The Government of India primarily channels sports funding and policy implementation through SAI, which operates under the Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports. SAI manages sports infrastructure, runs training centres, implements schemes (Khelo India, NSTC, TOPS) and disburses development funds. While IOA handles team selection for international events, SAI is the government's primary implementation arm.
elimination_hints: A ignores the central role of SAI; C wrongly assigns government funds to private sponsors; D wrongly involves the IOC in Indian domestic funding.
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE
